evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to scan US driver's licenses only and verify them for forged AAMVA barcodes and data inconsistency between the front and back of the document. Also reject expired IDs. On rejection for inconsistent data, extract and display the front review image from the verification result. On a successful scan, also check for and display the review image if available.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings targeting DriverLicense(region: .us) with rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes, rejectInconsistentData, and rejectExpiredIds flags, setIncludeImage for face and croppedDocument, and correct VerificationResult access in both callbacks.

assertions.text: Does NOT use AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier — verification is settings-driven
Handles .inconsistentData, .forgedAamvaBarcode, and .documentExpired in didReject with distinct messages
Uses idCapture.reset() when re-enabling capture after showing the verification result (or correctly re-enables isEnabled)

evals.prompt: The app already uses BarcodeCapture for scanning barcodes. Now add an ID scanning screen using IdCapture. Make sure the two modes don't interfere — the ID scanning screen should remove any existing mode before adding IdCapture.
evals.expected_output: Code that calls context.removeCurrentMode() (or context.removeAllModes()) before creating the IdCapture instance, ensuring BarcodeCapture and IdCapture don't co-exist unexpectedly.

assertions.text: Calls context.removeCurrentMode() or context.removeAllModes() before creating the IdCapture instance
Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) after the mode removal
Sets up IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and scanner
Implements IdCaptureListener with didCapture and didReject
Manages camera lifecycle in viewWillAppear / viewDidDisappear
